title: Observability, Capacity, Flow Control, Alarms, and SLOs · Contract Lens: Resource Alarm Flow Control
summary: A dashboard shows only queue depth, creates message-ID metric labels, ignores unacknowledged work and oldest age, and responds to memory alarms by adding publishers and increasing prefetch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the resource alarm flow control increment in messaging telemetry, capacity, and SLO evidence board. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace memory and disk alarms, connection blocking, client blocked events, broker flow control, local publish drain, and upstream load shedding.
Model arrival rate, service rate, concurrency, oldest age, burst, retry amplification, byte volume, and recovery time for capacity decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Resource Alarm Flow Control · Theory Resource Alarm Flow Control
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9242 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine backlog state, oldest age, SLO, and resource alarms into bounded operations action; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace memory and disk alarms, connection blocking, client blocked events, broker flow control, local publish drain, and upstream load shedding.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Resource Alarm Flow Control contract for the supplied data.
